title: Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Breast Cancer - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Breast Cancer 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions AIDS/HIV Breast Cancer Cancer Medicaid Recipients Older Adults Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details PINK LEMONADE PROJECT PINK LEMONADE PROJECT FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance to breast cancer patients who are experiencing hardship during treatment or recovery. Additional programs include support groups, peer mentorship, retreats, in-person and online events, screening support, cancer education for community health workers, and programs for those living with metastatic breast cancer. 1207 Washington Street Suite 125 Vancouver, WA 98660 Eligibility: There are no gender restrictions. To qualify, a person must: -Be receiving breast cancer treatment -Be living in or receiving care in Oregon -Be experiencing financial hardship during treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (360) 952-3814 5 Get Directions Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
